limitations.title: Greedy outperforms EquiRelief on reward
limitations.reason: When all 5 regions start with urgency=1.0 and similar need distributions, greedy and fair strategies converge to the same policy. The advantage of equity-aware RL is most visible when demand is severely imbalanced or when remote regions have low visibility.
limitations.evidence: Stress Test 1 (imbalanced demand): EquiRelief min-ratio > Greedy min-ratio.
limitations.severity: LOW — expected given uniform test conditions

limitations.title: Hinglish/Tanglish urgency F1 ~ 0.50
limitations.reason: mDeBERTa-v3 was pretrained on Devanagari Hindi and Tamil script. Romanized code-mixed text ("khana chahiye", "saapadu illai") does not map to the model's multilingual representations. No disaster-domain Hinglish/Tanglish training data exists anywhere.
limitations.evidence: English F1=0.879 vs Hinglish F1=0.517 — 36-point gap.
limitations.severity: HIGH — this is the documented research gap this project addresses

limitations.title: Region detection defaults to central (18.3% error)
limitations.observed: Region accuracy = 81.7% on manual test set
limitations.reason: NER model (Davlan mBERT) was trained on Wikipedia-domain text. Disaster-specific locations (shelter names, local landmarks, districts) are not well represented. Fallback keyword dict covers only ~30 place names.
limitations.evidence: CrisisNLP region distribution: 87% central before dict expansion.
limitations.severity: MEDIUM — mitigated by keyword dict, acceptable for academic prototype

limitations.title: Ablation shows slower convergence for complex variants
limitations.observed: At 500 ep: Standard DQN 90.8 > Full EquiRelief 80.7
limitations.reason: Double DQN + PER + n-Step requires more experience to converge because PER's importance weights and n-Step bootstrapping introduce variance early in training. At 1000 episodes Full EquiRelief overtakes.
limitations.evidence: 1000-episode ablation convergence trend: Full EquiRelief shows largest improvement from early to late training.
limitations.severity: LOW — expected behaviour, not a fundamental flaw

limitations.title: Simulated environment — not real disaster data
limitations.observed: RL environment uses synthetic Poisson demand arrivals
limitations.reason: No real-time disaster resource allocation dataset exists with ground truth optimal decisions. The Gymnasium environment is a reasonable approximation but does not capture all real-world complexity (supply chain constraints, transportation delays, etc.)
limitations.evidence: Standard practice in humanitarian AI literature.
limitations.severity: MEDIUM — acknowledged limitation of all RL-based humanitarian work

limitations.title: Cross-lingual dedup not empirically demonstrated
limitations.observed: No multi-language clusters found in manual test set
limitations.reason: The 180-sample test set contains manually created messages, not real cross-posted content. Real deployment would have the same flood reported in EN, HI, Hinglish simultaneously.
limitations.evidence: LaBSE embedding space analysis confirms multilingual similarity — stage functions correctly, empirical demonstration requires live data.
limitations.severity: LOW — architectural correctness verified, empirical demo deferred
